_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting exploratory data analysis, including distributional analysis, correlation analysis, anomaly detection, and translating EDA findings into modeling decisions.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Skill AI-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.448), AB-S-010 (AB, cosine: 0.421).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.621). Shared vocabulary: [analysis, skill]. NICE-only terms [performing, trend] vs WIDAI-only terms [anomaly, conducting, correlation, data, decisions, detection] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.899 (P=0.942, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.082 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Skill in performing trend analysis" and "Skill in conducting exploratory data analysis, including distributional analysis" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.458 < STS 0.621 (gap -0.162). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [performing, trend] and [anomaly, conducting, correlation] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.0%. Shared tokens: [analysis, skill]. NICE-only: [performing, trend]. WIDAI-only: [anomaly, conducting, correlation, data, decisions, detection].
